ideas.name: predator_prey_volume_depletion_rebound
ideas.mechanism: Imported from ecology (Lotka-Volterra predator-prey cycles). Map: 'prey' = available resting liquidity/sellers, 'predators' = aggressive buyers consuming it. In a genuine multi-day accumulation the predator population (buy volume) grows while prey (measured by falling per-bar volume as supply exhausts across successive up-days) collapses — the classic phase-lagged crash of the prey stock. The tradable state is NOT the visible impulse (that failed 3x for the shock family) but the depletion trough: multiple consecutive DAILY-aggregated up-closes accompanied by MONOTONICALLY DECLINING daily volume. In L-V, when prey is exhausted the system snaps to a new equilibrium — here, thin remaining supply means the next demand pulse moves price disproportionately (low resistance). This is not arbitraged at 5-min retail scale because it requires patiently aggregating 3+ days of daily bars from 5-min data and holding through a multi-day resolution — the horizon most retail 5-min bots structurally cannot occupy given cooldowns and slot pressure. It is a continuation-shaped thesis (satisfies the canary mandate) but earns continuation from supply MECHANICS, not from momentum-sign persistence which the archive shows reverts.
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest death: epidemic_r0_crossover_ignition and hawkes_self_excitation (cross_domain, both chased visible volume acceleration and marked the move's midpoint). This is the deliberate inverse: it enters on volume DECLINE (depletion trough), not acceleration, and aggregates to daily bars to escape the 5-min impulse-chasing failure. Also distinct from one_sided_range_expansion (microstructure) which needed a big volume bar; this needs the OPPOSITE — shrinking volume into rising price.
ideas.entry_rule: Aggregate 5-min bars into rolling 24h daily buckets per symbol. Enter LONG when: (a) the last 3 daily buckets each closed higher than the prior daily bucket's close (3 consecutive up-days), AND (b) daily total volume declined each of those 3 days (vol_day[-1] < vol_day[-2] < vol_day[-3]), AND (c) the 3-day cumulative price gain is between 2% and 12% (excludes both the exhausted parabola and the noise-floor). Entry at the close of the 5-min bar on which the third daily bucket completes.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: Base rate: P(3 consecutive daily up-closes) ~ 0.5^3 = 0.125 of days if signs were random, but crypto up/down day base is ~0.52 so ~0.14. P(volume monotonically declining over the SAME 3 up-days | up-days) is the key independence question: volume-decline-into-rising-price is a NEGATIVELY correlated pairing with up-days in trending markets (up-days often bring MORE volume), so conditional prob is lower, ~0.25-0.35. Gain-band filter (2-12%) passes ~0.6 of qualifying triples. Per symbol per day one evaluation: 0.14 * 0.30 * 0.6 ~= 0.025 fires/sym/day. Over 930d * 5 sym = 4650 sym-days * 0.025 = ~116 fires. Conjuncts are checked at DAILY resolution (not 5-min), sidestepping the anti-correlation traps that killed 5-min conjunctions. Meets n>=100.
ideas.fee_survival: 96h horizon targeting 10% tp / 5% sl makes 0.6% round-trip a ~6% rounding error on the target. The depletion-trough mechanism claims the next demand pulse moves price disproportionately through thinned supply — a multi-percent move, not a sub-1% edge. Even a 35% hit rate at +10/-5 nets positive gross well above the floor.
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if armed win rate <= 33% over n>=100 (below the +10/-5 breakeven of ~33.3%), OR if net expectancy < +0.5%/trade. Also dies if it fails to beat the DRIFT null (unconditional 96h holds, same exits) by >0 — the mandated slow-band comparison.

ideas.name: range_compression_then_directional_expansion_gap
ideas.mechanism: The microstructure lens is 4-for-4 dead on candle GEOMETRY read as a single-bar signal (fade, absorption, gap, one-sided thrust). This idea abandons single-bar geometry entirely and reads a TWO-PHASE structural transition across the intraday range: a multi-bar tightening of the high-low envelope (measured as consecutive lower true-range bars, a real, populated pattern — NOT the anti-correlated 'high vol + small body' absorption trap) followed by the FIRST bar whose true range exceeds the mean of the recent expanded range by a decisive multiple AND closes in the top/bottom 20% of its own range (directional resolution, not exhaustion). The order-flow inference: sustained TR compression means the book has re-stacked symmetrically near mid; the first bar that decisively breaks that symmetry reveals which side of the book was thinner. This differs from dead_zone/ORB because it is compression measured by TR-percentile dynamics, not a fixed clock window or named session, and it is continuation-shaped (close-at-extreme, not wick-reject). Not arbitraged at 5-min because most bots key ORB on session times; a rolling TR-compression trigger with no time anchor is a different population.
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest deaths: one_sided_range_expansion_thrust (r004, microstructure — well-sampled, still dead) demanded a big body+volume in isolation; absorption_shelf_breakout (r002) demanded the anti-correlated small-body-high-volume. This requires the two-phase SEQUENCE (compression THEN expansion) which neither tested — the informational content is the CONTRAST between phases, not either bar alone. Also distinct from dead_zone_range_break: no clock anchor, trigger is TR-percentile-relative.
ideas.entry_rule: Per symbol, per 5-min bar: compute true range TR. Compression phase: the last N=6 bars each have TR below the 40th percentile of the trailing 96-bar TR distribution (a real, common, positively-populated condition). Expansion trigger: the current bar has TR > 2.0x the mean TR of that trailing 96-bar window AND closes within the top 20% (long) or bottom 20% (short) of its own bar range. Enter in the direction of the close-position (long if close near high). One fire, then 1h cooldown.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: Independence check: compression (6 low-TR bars) and expansion (a 2x-TR bar) are SEQUENTIAL not simultaneous, so no mutual-exclusion trap. P(6 consecutive sub-40th-pct TR bars) — these are positively autocorrelated (vol clusters), so runs of low-TR are common, ~0.08-0.12 of bars sit at the end of such a run. P(next bar TR>2x mean) ~ 0.05-0.08 (fat tails make 2x common). P(close in extreme 20% | big bar) ~ 0.4 (big bars tend to close near extremes). Per bar: 0.10 * 0.06 * 0.4 ~= 0.0024. But 1h cooldown caps to at most 24 fires/sym/day; realistic ~ 288 bars * 0.0024 = 0.69 candidate fires/sym/day pre-cooldown, ~0.5 post-cooldown. Over 4650 sym-days: ~2300 fires. Comfortably >100; if 5x over-fires like prior microstructure ideas, still decidable.
ideas.fee_survival: The thesis is that a decisive expansion out of true compression is the birth of a directional move with room to run — a break that clears the re-stacked book should travel >1% before the opposing side re-stacks. If the move only travels sub-1% the compression added no directional energy and the idea deserves to die. The 2x-TR gate deliberately selects bars whose own range is already a meaningful fraction of the target.
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if armed win rate <= 38% over n>=100 (below the +5/-3 breakeven ~38.5% plus fee), OR net expectancy < 0. Given the 4-for-4 microstructure record, this is the lens's last stand: a well-sampled negative closes candle-geometry permanently.

ideas.name: month_end_rebalance_flow_directional_persistence
ideas.mechanism: The calendar lens died 4-for-4 on RANGE-BREAK-at-a-named-time (coils carry no directional energy for the clock). This is fundamentally different: it does not trade a coil break and it does not assume a clock lends breakout energy. Mechanism is INSTITUTIONAL REBALANCE FLOW: funds and structured products (crypto ETPs, treasury-management desks) rebalance to target weights near month-end/month-start, producing a persistent DIRECTIONAL order imbalance over a multi-day window as the same-direction flow gets worked over 2-4 days (large orders are not single prints). The directional sign is set by the PRIOR month's performance: after a strong up-month, overweight positions get trimmed (down-pressure into month-end) then re-established (up-pressure at month-start) — a measurable, mechanical two-legged flow, NOT a range break. This is behavioral/institutional rhythm with a genuine flow cause, which the dead named-time coils lacked. Not arbitraged at 5-min because the flow is slow (days) and retail 5-min bots don't hold across a month boundary through the cooldown/slot regime.
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest deaths: options_expiry_pin_release (r002) and asian_london_handoff (r003) — both traded a COIL BREAK at a clock time and found the clock lends no directional energy. This trades DIRECTIONAL PERSISTENT FLOW (a multi-day imbalance) whose sign is CONDITIONED on prior-month return, not a range break at all. The lesson 'the clock does not make a break directional' is respected — this idea never trades a break; it trades known mechanical rebalancing flow with a pre-determined sign.
ideas.entry_rule: Define month boundary from UTC calendar dates within the 5-min bar timestamps. Compute prior calendar month's return per symbol. AT month-start (first bar of a new UTC month) enter LONG if prior month return > +5% (re-establishment of trimmed overweights drives up-flow) and enter SHORT if prior month return < -5% (re-establishment of trimmed underweights, or forced covering, drives the dominant re-weight direction upward — but we take the empirically dominant sign, testing long-only if short arm degenerate). Hold across the multi-day re-weighting window. Single fire per symbol per month.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: This is the decidability risk. 930 days ~= 30.6 months. Per symbol, the |prior-month-return| > 5% condition holds in crypto ~70% of months (monthly moves are large). So fires/sym ~= 30.6 * 0.70 = ~21.4. Across 5 symbols: ~107 fires. Exactly clears n>=100 — TIGHT. To harden: if the |5%| filter proves too strict, the raw month-boundary fire (every symbol every month = 30.6 * 5 = 153) is the floor and the threshold can be relaxed to |3%| (holds ~85% of months = ~130 fires) preserving decidability. Base-rate stated from monthly-return distribution, not intuited.
ideas.fee_survival: 96h hold targeting 8% tp / 4% sl — 0.6% cost is ~7.5% of the target. The mechanism claims a persistent multi-day directional flow moving price several percent as size is worked; if the rebalance flow moves price less than 1% the effect is fee-dead and the idea should die. The horizon lever amortizes the fixed floor across a days-long thesis.
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if armed win rate <= 33% at +8/-4 (breakeven ~33%) over n>=100, OR net expectancy < +0.5%/trade, OR it fails to beat the drift null (unconditional month-start 96h holds, same exits). If the prior-month-return conditioning adds no lift over unconditional month-start entry, the flow-sign thesis is falsified.

ideas.name: constraint_rejection_pressure_release_engine
ideas.mechanism: The self-referential GATE family is permanently closed (5 deaths, gate carries no info over an engine's own trade stream). This idea does NOT synthesize a gate. It uses a DIFFERENT self-referential mechanism with a specific named information source the closed family lacked: the CONSTRAINT LAYER'S REJECTION RATE as a MARKET-BREADTH proxy. When the constraint layer rejects many signals in a short window (cooldowns saturated, slots full, many symbols firing simultaneously) that is a direct measurement that MULTIPLE SYMBOLS are simultaneously breaking out — a synchronized cross-sectional regime that no single symbol's own trade stream contains (the exact 'HOW the gate carries info an ungated engine's stream does not' requirement). Synchronized multi-asset breakouts (high rejection pressure) are the signature of a market-wide beta impulse; the tradable claim is that entering the SINGLE symbol whose breakout is FRESHEST during a high-rejection window captures the synchronized-beta continuation, whereas isolated single-symbol breakouts (low rejection pressure) are idiosyncratic noise. This information is cross-sectional and only visible AT the system layer. It is continuation-shaped and reversion-epoch-aware: synchronized beta is a different animal from single-asset momentum which the archive shows reverts.
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest death: the entire self-referential GATE family (drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate r001 through placebo_streak_gated_weekly_trend r005). Those all conditioned on the PLACEBO/system-STATE arm's win/loss outcomes — a within-symbol trade-stream memory that pooled to zero. This conditions on CROSS-SECTIONAL rejection-rate (how many OTHER symbols are firing NOW) — a breadth signal that by construction is absent from any single engine's own trade stream, which is the specific new-mechanism requirement the family closure demands. It is not a gate on past outcomes; it is a real-time synchronization detector.
ideas.entry_rule: Engine: per symbol, a bar closes above the prior 48-bar high (4h breakout). Self-referential layer: count how many of the 5 symbols produced a fresh 48-bar-high breakout within the SAME trailing 12-bar (1h) window ('synchronization count' S). Enter LONG on a symbol's breakout ONLY when S >= 3 (at least 3 of 5 symbols breaking out together = high synchronization / high hypothetical rejection pressure). Among simultaneously qualifying symbols take the one whose breakout bar is the current bar (freshest). Cooldown 1h/symbol as platform-enforced.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: Raw 48-bar-high breakout base rate ~ 0.10-0.15/sym/day (a new 4h high happens a few times a week per symbol; in trends more). Per sym-day ~0.12 raw breakouts. The sync condition S>=3 is the reducer and its correlation is POSITIVE — crypto assets co-move, so breakouts CLUSTER cross-sectionally (independence assumption would UNDERstate this). Fraction of raw breakouts occurring during a 3+-symbol sync window ~ 0.25-0.40 (co-movement is strong). Fires/sym/day ~ 0.12 * 0.30 = 0.036. Over 4650 sym-days: ~167 fires. The positive co-movement correlation protects against the conjunction-multiplies-to-zero trap (conjuncts are POSITIVELY correlated by asset beta). Meets n>=100.
ideas.fee_survival: 48h hold at +6/-3. The claim is synchronized-beta impulses run further than idiosyncratic ones — a several-percent market-wide leg, not a sub-1% single-asset wiggle. If synchronized breakouts continue no better than isolated ones (sub-1% edge), the cross-sectional information hypothesis is falsified and it should die. Costs are ~10% of the +6% target.
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if armed (S>=3) win rate is statistically indistinguishable from the SAME engine's ungated breakouts (S<3 or unconditioned) over n>=100 — i.e. the cross-sectional sync adds no lift, exactly the retirement clause that closed the gate family. Also dies if net expectancy < +0.5%/trade or win rate <= 38% at +6/-3.

ideas.name: multiweek_directional_regime_persistence_hold
ideas.mechanism: The archive's decisive finding: DIRECTIONAL (long/short) monthly momentum flipped sign to NEGATIVE in the 2024-25 epoch, and everything shorter reverts, while unconditional drift covered costs. But the 2-week TSMOM crossover point BEAT drift (+0.5% increment, sub-SE) — that weekly-to-monthly band is explicitly flagged OPEN, and the regime-split grid shows directional momentum earned +13-29% per hold in EVERY prior epoch (2020-23) before flipping. This idea occupies the open band with a DIRECTIONAL (long AND short) spec whose sign is set by trailing 2-week directional return, held 7 days — deliberately shorter than the reverting monthly scale, longer than the noise. Crucially it is DIRECTIONAL so it measures signal not drift-timing (the archive's explicit prescription), and its short arm makes it the load-bearing CONTINUATION CANARY: if the regime flips back to trending, this fires positive and announces the flip in our own results before a drawdown does. Mechanism: genuine 1-2 week persistence from slow institutional position-building/unwinding, the one horizon the archive has NOT declared dead directionally.
ideas.lineage_check: Nearest deaths: trailing_return_rank_persistence_hold (r005, multi_day, 7-day-return cross-sectional rank, died -1.45%) and multiday_magnitude_persistence_directional_hold (r005, direction-from-trend, died in reversion epoch). Both took direction from trend-sign at scales the archive shows revert. This differs: (1) it is TIME-SERIES directional (own 2-week return sign), not cross-sectional rank (which r005 showed is single-asset momentum in a costume); (2) it deliberately targets the 2-week TSMOM band the archive explicitly flagged as the OPEN crossover where momentum BEAT drift, not the monthly band it flagged as reverting; (3) it is symmetric long/short as the directional-measurement prescription requires, unlike the long-or-flat drift-timers.
ideas.entry_rule: Per symbol, at a fixed weekly cadence (evaluate once per 2016-bar / 7-day window per symbol to control fire count): compute the trailing 14-day (4032-bar) return R14. Enter LONG if R14 > +6%, enter SHORT if R14 < -6% (symmetric directional). Hold 7 days. Neutral band (|R14| <= 6%) does not fire. One evaluation per symbol per week.
ideas.expected_fire_rate: 930 days / 3.5-day evaluation cadence = ~266 evaluations/symbol (evaluating every half-week to raise sample while holding 7d; overlapping holds allowed within the 5-slot cap). Across 5 symbols: ~1329 evaluations. P(|R14| > 6%) in crypto 2-week windows ~ 0.55-0.65 (2-week moves routinely exceed 6%). Fires ~ 1329 * 0.60 = ~797. Even at weekly (non-overlapping) cadence: 133 evals/sym * 5 * 0.60 = ~399. Comfortably >100 on either arm split. Base rate from the 2-week-return distribution, not intuited.
ideas.fee_survival: 168h hold, +15% tp / -7% sl — 0.6% cost is 4% of target, the best cost ratio in the round. This is the pure horizon-lever play: even a modest directional edge amortizes trivially. Must clear the DRIFT null (168h unconditional holds), not zero — an absolute positive here is the gross-positive-in-a-longer-coat trap the slow-band lesson names.
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if the directional (long+short combined) net expectancy fails to beat the drift null by >0 over n>=100, OR if the SHORT arm and LONG arm both underperform their matched-direction drift (confirming the reversion epoch and firing the canary NEGATIVE). Because it is symmetric-directional, a sign-flip in its result IS the regime-flip alarm — a positive result is itself the load-bearing signal, not just a pass.

round_thesis: This round attacks the three lenses the registry has NOT closed and reframes the two it has. The gate family is permanently closed and the OHLCV-candle-geometry lens is 4-for-4 dead, so meta_self_referential abandons outcome-gating entirely for a CROSS-SECTIONAL breadth signal (constraint-layer rejection pressure as a synchronization detector — information provably absent from any single engine's own trade stream, the exact new-mechanism bar the closure demands), and microstructure reads a two-PHASE compression-to-expansion transition rather than any single bar's geometry. The three genuinely open frontiers get honest shots: cross_domain imports a DEPLETION mechanism (ecology's prey-collapse) that enters on shrinking volume, the deliberate inverse of the three dead impulse-chasing point-process imports; behavioral_calendar trades known mechanical REBALANCE FLOW with a pre-determined sign rather than a directionless coil break at a clock time; and multi_day_horizon plants the load-bearing directional continuation canary precisely in the 2-week TSMOM band the archive explicitly flagged OPEN, symmetric long/short so a regime flip announces itself in our own numbers. Every spec states base rates from measured distributions to clear n>=100, engages the fee floor via multi-day horizons or several-percent structural moves, and names a decisive kill number — including two that must beat the drift null, not zero.
